demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage Plan (Blue Cross); cross-referenced facility face sheet with Atlantis and corrected primary phone number for son/POA to ensure pulse call connectivity.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirmed goal is Home Discharge with skilled nursing and physical therapy for post-CABG sternal precaution monitoring.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: Patient has not heard of the company. Q2: Knows we help with the transition but unclear on specifics. Q3: SW mentioned a 'home helper' but no detailed explanation. Q4: Patient is extremely anxious about chest pain management and 'something popping' in his chest; escalated anxiety to SW.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient meeting PT goals; sternal incision shows no signs of infection. SW projects Friday discharge.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into patient's personal smartphone; program flyer with Day 1 expectations hand-delivered to patient's wife.
hha_confirmation_status: In Place: HHA (Heartland Home Care) confirmed SOC date for Friday 10/27, assigned Nurse Maria V., and provided a 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM arrival window.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday discharge is locked and Heartland has all clinical orders. Call 2 (to Wife): Verified she knows Nurse Maria is arriving Friday morning and knows to call the HHA office if the window is missed.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient/family 24 hours prior to discharge. Home is ready, oxygen concentrator delivered, and sternal precautions understood. No new clinical barriers identified.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered Discharge Date (10/27) and first visit date (10/27) into Atlantis tracking module.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for Friday 10/27 at 2:30 PM (after HHA 'In Place' confirmation) to ensure the nurse arrives first.
narrative_summary: Managed the transition for a post-CABG patient, identifying high anxiety during the Connection Intake which was escalated to the Social Worker. Executed a full Confidence Loop, verifying HHA status as 'In Place' with a confirmed nurse and time window before scheduling the MA. Despite a perfect handoff and confirmed 24-hour pulse call, the HHA nurse failed to arrive for the Day 1 visit, resulting in a transition breakdown.

format_1_state_log.event_description: HHA confirmation received for Friday SOC window.
Day 1 Home No-Show: HHA nurse Maria V. fails to arrive by 12:00 PM.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient expresses fear of incision reopening during Connection Intake.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated patient anxiety regarding sternal precautions to the SW and facility nurse.
Conducted Confidence Audit calls to both the SW and the patient's wife to verify Day 1 arrival window.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA nurse fails to show for the scheduled 10:00 AM window.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Called HHA intake coordinator to identify the blocker and flagged the no-show to the SW.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Advise the wife to wait until the end of the day to see if the nurse arrives and then call the HHA's after-hours line if no one shows.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the HHA's internal scheduling autonomy but fails to provide the high-touch coordination required during the critical 24-hour post-discharge window.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Immediately call the HHA intake coordinator to identify why the nurse missed the window, then update the SW and family on the new ETA or agency failure.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This follows the Deep_Dive_HHA_Barrier protocol, transforming a vague no-show into an actionable escalation for the SW while keeping the family informed.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Drive to the patient's home to perform a visual check of the sternal incision and help the patient with their cardiac medications since the nurse is late.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is a severe clinical overstep; the PN is not permitted to perform clinical assessments or manage medications, regardless of HHA performance.
